top_signals.title: Kevin Warsh names members of his Federal Reserve task forces, including Marc Andreessen, Doug McMillon
top_signals.summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh on Thursday released names of the experts who will comprise five task forces to examine the institution's operations.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Music tycoon Simon Cowell sued by prominent City lawyer
top_signals.summary: City lawyer Ian Rosenblatt has launched legal action in London’s High Court against music mogul Simon Cowell over alleged withheld fees. The lawsuit stems from the lawyer stepping down as director of Syco Entertainment, Cowell’s production company, and as an adviser to the talent show judge. Quinn Emanuel’s partner Gregory Pantlin and senior associate Emma [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: The United States produced more crude oil than any other country in 2025
top_signals.summary: The United States remained the world's largest crude oil producer in 2025, according to our International Energy Statistics database, extending a streak that began in 2018 when the United States overtook Russia to become the world's leading producer.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Ukrainian ground robot maker doubles production, eyes ventures with foreign partners
top_signals.summary: “The most important mission that we have now is the robotization of the battlefield, because only this will allow our country to win the war.”
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Vanessa Lawrence, Andrew Halford and Iain Mackay reappointed on UK Government Investments Board
top_signals.summary: His Majesty’s Treasury (HMT) has today announced the reappointment of Dr Vanessa Lawrence CB, Andrew Halford and Iain Mackay as Non-Executive Directors of UK Government Investments (UKGI).
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: U.S. exports of crude oil and petroleum products reached record in April
top_signals.summary: U.S. petroleum exports reached a record in April as disruptions to international crude oil and refined product flows through the Strait of Hormuz increased global demand for U.S. exports. Exports increased to 13.6 million barrels per day (b/d) in April, 15% more than the previous record set in March.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

Aave rolls out vaults for yield-hungry fintech investors
Age verification is the surveillance nobody voted for
Hong Kong regulator orders new anti-phishing measures for crypto platforms
top_signals.summary: Hong Kong’s regulator has ordered crypto platforms and online brokers to meet newly issued phishing-resistant login requirements within the next 12 months.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Sky’s ITV takeover could be tonic for Premier League media rights value
top_signals.summary: Sky’s proposed takeover of ITV is widely expected to mean more sport on free-to-air channels in the UK, but wider consolidation in the media market could also have a significant impact on the value of sports broadcast rights. A combined Sky and ITV would, if approved by regulators, create an even more powerful player in [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.summary: Gallery, Mini Sessions, and Two-Way SMS bring the full client journey into a single photographer-focused platform for the first time

alerts.title: The 250-year history of U.S. energy consumption
alerts.summary: Over the 250-year history of our nation, energy consumption has evolved from wood use in the 18th and 19th centuries to today's use of modern renewable, hydrocarbon, and nuclear technology. In 2025, total energy used in the United States was 96 quadrillion British thermal units (quads), up 2% from 2024, but below 2007's record 99 quads. Petroleum was the most-used energy source last year, followed closely by natural gas. Use of renewable, coal, and nuclear energy each made up about 9% of total energy use.
